extra.message: User input from sqli parameter 'username' is directly concatenated into SQL query string using f-string. This allows SQL injection attacks via the username parameter.
User input from xss parameter 'username' is directly reflected in JSON response without escaping. This allows reflected XSS attacks when the response is interpreted as HTML.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation

extra.message: User input from lfi parameter 'filename' is directly passed to open() function without validation or sanitization. This allows path traversal attacks to read arbitrary files.

cwe: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory

extra.message: User input from rfi parameter 'imagelink' is directly passed to requests.get() function without validation. This allows Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) attacks to make requests to internal resources or arbitrary external URLs.
User input from ssti parameter 'mathexp' is directly concatenated into a Jinja2 Template string. This allows Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI) attacks leading to arbitrary code execution.

cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code

extra.message: HTTP request header 'Host' is directly reflected in HTML response without escaping. This allows Host Header Injection attacks to inject arbitrary HTML content or perform cache poisoning.

cwe: CWE-113: Improper Neutralization of CRLF Sequences in HTTP Headers

extra.message: User-controlled vuln values are directly concatenated into SQL INSERT statement without proper escaping or parameterization. This allows SQL injection via the vuln tuple values.
